name: Posted registry results do not establish metformin frailty-prevention benefit
summary: The NCT02570672 registry adds direct frailty-prevention results, but the posted data do not provide a clear functional benefit signal.
statement: The NCT02570672 registry posted results for a 141-participant metformin frailty-prevention trial in high-risk older adults; the reported monthly frailty-index rate favored metformin directionally, while the Fried frailty score increased faster in the metformin group than placebo, so the registry results do not establish clear functional benefit.
population_or_model: Community-dwelling adults aged 65 to 90 years with prediabetes or impaired glucose tolerance and without baseline frailty
quantitative_note: Posted results show frailty-index monthly rate -0.0002 (SE 0.0002) for metformin versus 0.0002 (SE 0.0002) for placebo, and Fried frailty score monthly rate 0.0289 (SE 0.0049) for metformin versus 0.0164 (SE 0.0058) for placebo.
caveats: This finding is based on registry-posted results rather than a peer-reviewed outcomes paper.
The registry excerpt does not provide statistical comparison details in the extracted outcome table.
Adverse-event reporting includes more diarrhea and nausea events in the metformin arm, which matters for preventive use.
